examples: The reforms were carried out with an almost messianic zeal.
The book proved such a success that the authors followed it up with "The Messianic Legacy."
There is no Messianic hope expressed in the book.
In a way then, all the messianic promise of "Kony 2012" is eerily prescient.
As is his wont, Mr Blair tends to be more messianic about this than most politicians.
